assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves the existing trustify-backend entry in the Repository Registry unchanged (same Role, Serena Instance, Path values as in claude-md-configured.md)
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md line 7: '| trustify-backend | Rust backend service | serena_backend | /home/user/trustify-backend |' matches claude-md-configured.md line 16 exactly.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md adds a new trustify-ui entry to the Repository Registry with serena_ui as the Serena Instance
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md line 8: '| trustify-ui | TypeScript frontend | serena_ui | /home/user/trustify-ui |' — new row present with serena_ui as Serena Instance.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves all existing Jira Configuration fields unchanged — Project key remains TC, Cloud ID, Feature issue type ID remain as in claude-md-configured.md
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md lines 12-16 match claude-md-configured.md lines 20-24 exactly: Project key: TC, Cloud ID: 2b9e35e3-6bd3-4cec-b838-f4249ee02432, Feature issue type ID: 10142, Git Pull Request custom field: customfield_10875, GitHub Issue custom field: customfield_10747.

assertion_results.text: outputs/changes-log.md explicitly identifies which entries were preserved (existing) and which were added (new)
assertion_results.evidence: changes-log.md has '## Preserved (no changes)' section (lines 7-28) listing trustify-backend row, all Jira fields, Code Intelligence content, and Bug Configuration as preserved. '## Added (new entries)' section (lines 29-35) lists trustify-ui row and serena_ui limitation as added.

assertion_results.text: outputs/discovery-log.md notes that serena_backend was already configured and serena_ui is newly discovered
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md lines 19-20: '1. `serena_backend` — already in Repository Registry, skipped' and '2. `serena_ui` — NOT in Repository Registry, needs configuration'.

assertion_results.text: The existing Code Intelligence section content (naming convention, example, serena_backend limitations) is preserved — only new serena_ui content is added
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md lines 20-34: naming convention ('Tools are prefixed by Serena instance name'), serena_backend example code block, and serena_backend limitation ('rust-analyzer may take 30-60 seconds to index on first use') all preserved verbatim from original. Only addition is line 34: '- `serena_ui`: No known limitations'.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves the existing '## Bug Configuration' section with Bug issue type ID (10001), Bug template (docs/bug-template.md), and Bug-to-Task link type (Blocks) unchanged — the skill reports Bug Configuration is up to date
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md lines 37-40: '## Bug Configuration' with Bug issue type ID: 10001, Bug template: docs/bug-template.md, Bug-to-Task link type: Blocks — all match original. discovery-log.md line 51: 'Result: "Bug Configuration is up to date" — skipped.'

assertion_results.text: The skill offers Security Configuration opt-in since the existing CLAUDE.md has no '## Security Configuration' section, and respects the user's decline — no Security Configuration section appears in the output
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md lines 55-58: 'Section does not exist in existing configuration. Asked user whether to enable security triage. Simulated user interaction: User declined to enable security triage. Result: Security Configuration skipped.' claude-md-result.md contains no '## Security Configuration' section. changes-log.md line 40 confirms: 'User declined to enable security triage — section not created'.
